subject: usbhid: apply jspoll for gamepad usage as well.
author: Sorgelig

impact.effect_if_absent: USB HID devices with top-level usage HID_GD_GAMEPAD would not be covered by the hid_jspoll_interval module parameter override; they would use their hardware-default USB interrupt polling interval rather than the MiSTer-configured jspoll value.

notes: Original fork commit (f0982bf2c) included 3 pr_info() debug statements that were dropped in the carried patch (0025-usbhid-jspoll-gamepad.patch). The carried patch keeps only the functional change: adding `case HID_GD_GAMEPAD:` to fall through to the same jspoll interval override as HID_GD_JOYSTICK. Vanilla 6.18.38 still lacks this case and thus does not apply jspoll overrides to gamepad devices. The feature is a straightforward enhancement: HID_GD_GAMEPAD has existed in the kernel since 2005 (include/linux/hid.h) and is used in other HID subsystems (hid-input.c), but was omitted from the usbhid jspoll override logic when that feature was added in 2017 (commit 933bfe4d271ef). The carried patch correctly forward-ports this fix from v5.15 to v6.18.38. No userspace coupling detected; this is a kernel-level USB polling optimization.
